clues.title: The Founding Seating Chart
clues.description: The 1882 wager's seating chart, framed and yellowing. Three deaths are annotated in Aster's own hand — chairs 2, 3, and 5, each with a date.

clues.title: Three Dates in Five Weeks
clues.description: Heart, fall, fever — three causes of death inked beneath the chart. Each interval between them is shorter than the last.

clues.title: The Founding Wager
clues.description: In 1882 the twelve members swore that whoever of them lived longest would take the Hampstead observatory and everything its dome conceals. Aster confirms the wager still stands.

clues.title: The Moved Sash Weights
clues.description: Chair 5's fatal fall was ruled an accident, but the window's sash weight channel shows fresh tool-marks concealed beneath new paint. The drop was prepared, not chanced upon.

clues.title: Chair 2's Tonic
clues.description: A patent tonic bottle found in chair 2's rooms, its dregs laced with enough arsenic to read as poisoning. Bought, dosed, and drunk by his own hand — the wrong kind of guilty.

clues.title: The Founder's Obituary
clues.description: Chair 1, the Society's founding secretary, died 'of age' in 1885 — three years after the comet. The date fits the pattern as its first, earliest collection.

clues.title: Sloane's Ledger
clues.description: An hour alone with Sloane's private accounts turns up ruinous debts to a Court of Smoke moneylender — and a run of payments that stops, abruptly, the week chair five died.

clues.title: Weir's Account
clues.description: Cornered and out of road, Weir admits he was paid for one death only, staged with care to sit inside a pattern he did not create.

clues.title: The Halloway Diary
clues.description: Chair 3's last weeks, in his own hand: sleeping through daylight, waking at dusk, and twice speaking of 'his number coming round.'

clues.title: The Skipped Chair
clues.description: Corven was called from the eyepiece on the comet night by a misdirected telegram and never took his turn to look. The pattern of deaths has passed over his chair ever since.

clues.title: The Private Observation Log
clues.description: The Society's own record of the comet night, kept apart from the published account. One page, three entries from the crucial night, has been razored cleanly from the spine.

clues.title: The Razored Page
clues.description: The missing night's true entry, recovered from the founder's unread Bible: not the comet, but an object crossing between it and the Earth, sketched as an angular, wrong-jointed mark the pencil seems to have struggled to hold steady.

clues.title: Ost's Recomputation
clues.description: Ost's unreported recalculation of the object's orbit: it is periodic, not a passing comet, and its next perihelion is uncomfortably close at hand.

clues.title: The Worked Dome
clues.description: Briggs's private almanac of nights the dome is 'worked' though no one enters it — dates he has learned to mark down, and increasingly, to avoid.

clues.title: The Warm Refractor
clues.description: On a marked night, the great refractor is warm to the touch — warm as a kettle recently off the boil, in a dome that stood unheated for hours before you entered it.

clues.title: Aster's Purchase
clues.description: The formal assignment of chair 11's outstanding debts to Aster, dated the day before he engaged your services.
